Definition: Measures the number of violent events that happen as a result of battles or explosions/remote violence between state forces and non-state actors. Battles are violent interactions between two politically organised armed groups and can occur between armed and organised state, non-state and external groups and in combination therein. Explosions/Remote violence are one-sided violent events in which the tool for engaging in conflict creates asymmetry by taking away the ability of the target to respond. State forces are defined as collective actors that are recognised to perform government functions, including military and police, over a given territory. Non-state actors include rebels, political and communal/identity militias and external forces.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - AbsArmedConf. Original data source/s - ACLED/UCDP

Definition: Measures the number of violent events against civilians perpetrated by government forces and the levels of political violence in a country.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - AbsViolCiv. Original data source/s - ACLED/PTS

Definition: Estimates the total stock of internally displaced persons (IDPs) due to conflict and violence in a given year, calculated by MIF per 100,000 population using the World Population Prospects by UNDESA. IDMC defines IDPs as “persons or groups of persons who have been forced or obliged to flee or to leave their homes or places of habitual residence, in particular as a result of or in order to avoid the effects of armed conflict, situations of generalised violence, violations of human rights or natural or human-made disasters, and who have not crossed an internationally recognised state border.”
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - AbsForcedMig. Original data source/s - IDMC/UNHCR

Variable Name: Absence of Human Trafficking & Forced Labour
Definition: Assesses the extent of human trafficking activity. Human trafficking includes activity, means and purpose, and reflects all stages of the illicit activity, from recruitment and transfer, to harbouring and receipt of persons and it does not require the movement of persons. In line with the Protocol to Prevent, Suppress and Punish Trafficking in Persons, Especially Women and Children, supplementing the United Nations Convention against Transnational Organized Crime (UNTOC), exploitation shall include, at a minimum, the exploitation or the prostitution of others or other forms of sexual exploitation, forced labour or services, slavery or practices similar to slavery, servitude or the removal of organs.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - AbHumTraForcLab. Original data source/s - ENACT/V-DEM

Definition: Assesses the extent of organised crime in a country based on the presence of criminal actors and criminal markets.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - AbsCrim. Original data source/s - ENACT/WHO

Definition: Assesses the extent to which citizens feel safe walking in their neighbourhood.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - PubPercSaf. Original data source/s - AFR

Variable Name: Executive Compliance with the Rule of Law
Definition: Assesses the extent to which members of the executive (the head of state, the head of government and cabinet ministers) respect the constitution.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - ExecCompRoL. Original data source/s - V-DEM/WJP

Definition: Assesses the extent to which the high court, lower courts and judges are independent.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - JudSys. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/V-DEM

Definition: Assesses the extent to which justice is accessible and affordable.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - JudProc. Original data source/s - V-DEM/WJP

Definition: Assesses the extent to which laws, policies and practices guarantee equal treatments to various distinct groups, including ethnic, racial, religious, gender, LGBT+ and other relevant groups and the extent to which these groups are able to exercise their human rights with full equality before the law.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - EqTreatLaw. Original data source/s - FH/WJP

Definition: Assesses the extent to which the police are reliable and there is a functioning system for investigating and sanctioning police misconduct.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - LawEnf. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/WEF/WJP

Definition: Assesses the extent to which government authorities ensure well-defined rights of private property and regulate the acquisition, benefits, use and sale of property.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - PropRights. Original data source/s - BS/V-DEM/WJP

Variable Name: Public Perception of the Rule of Law
Definition: Assesses the extent to which citizens feel the head of the executive does not ignore the courts and laws.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - PubPercROL. Original data source/s - AFR

Definition: Assesses the extent to which there is a working separation of powers (checks and balances). It Assesses the extent to which there is a clear separation of powers with mutual checks and balances and the basic configuration and operation of the separation of powers. It includes the subjection of state power to the law.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - InstChecBal. Original data source/s - BS/V-DEM/WJP

Definition: Assesses the extent to which democratically elected political representatives have the effective power to govern, or the extent to which there are veto powers and political enclaves. It Assesses the extent to which individuals or groups have the power to undermine democratic procedures without questioning the system as such. Veto powers can come from the military, the clergy, landowners, business elites, and external actors, among others. They can 'veto' the results of democratic decisions or retain prerogatives that cannot be touched by democratically elected officeholders (e.g., nominating the commander-in-chief of the armed forces).
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - AbsUnInfGov. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/BS/FH

Definition: Assesses the extent to which there are effective mechanisms for citizens to make complaints and there is a right to petition and civic engagement.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - CivCheckBal. Original data source/s - BS/V-DEM/WJP

Definition: Assesses the amount and timeliness of budget information that governments make publicly available in eight key budget documents in accordance with international good practice standards.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - DiscPubRec. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/IBP/WJP

Definition: Assesses the extent to which publicly available records, legislative information and records of state-owned companies are accessible.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - AccPubRec. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/UNDESA/WJP

Definition: Assesses the extent to which citizens feel the head of the executive does not ignore parliament and does not just do what they want.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - PubPercAcc. Original data source/s - AFR

Definition: Assesses the extent to which the government successfully contains corruption. It Assesses the extent to which adequate institutional arrangements exist to implement an anti-corruption policy and if they successfully contribute to an effective prosecution of corruption. It also Assesses the extent to which the government implements and monitors the following integrity mechanisms: auditing of state spending, regulation of party financing, citizen and media access to information, accountability of officeholders including asset declarations, conflict of interest rules, codes of conduct and transparent public procurement system.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - AntiCorrMech. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/BS

Variable Name: Absence of Corruption in State Institutions
Definition: Assesses the extent to which the judicial system is free from bribes and officials in the judicial branch do not use their public office for private gain.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - AbsCorrStateInst. Original data source/s - V-DEM/WJP

Variable Name: Absence of Corruption in the Public Sector
Definition: Assesses the extent to which public funds are not illegally being diverted and public sector employees do not engage in embezzlement and theft.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - AbsCorrPubSect. Original data source/s - V-DEM/WEF/WJP

Definition: Assesses the extent to which bids from competing contractors, suppliers, or vendors are invited through open advertising of the scope, specifications, and terms of the proposed contract, and the criteria by which the bids are evaluated are available for scrutiny.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - PubProcuProc. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/WJP

Variable Name: Absence of Corruption in the Private Sector
Definition: Assesses how common it is for firms to make undocumented extra payments or bribes connected with (a) imports and exports; (b) public utilities; (c) annual tax payments; (d) awarding of public contracts and licenses; (e) obtaining favourable judicial decisions. is an aggregate of these five underlying variables and the calculation is done by MIF.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - AbsCorrPrivSect. Original data source/s - WB/WEF

Definition: Assesses the extent to which citizens feel the head of the executive, members of parliament and the police are not involved in corruption.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - PubPercCorr. Original data source/s - AFR

Definition: Assesses the extent to which citizens are able to associate freely with their peers in public, they are free to express dissent in public settings and are free to participate in civic and political organisations.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - FreeAssocAss. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/FH

Definition: Assesses the extent to which any political parties are banned, barriers to party formation exist and the opposition parties are independent from the ruling regime.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - PolPlur. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/V-DEM

Definition: Assesses the extent to which civil society and non-governmental organisations (NGOs) can establish and operate freely.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - CivSocSpace. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/V-DEM

Definition: Assesses the extent to which elections are free and fair and held regularly without irregularities and/or violence.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - DemElec. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/CDD-Ghana/V-DEM

Definition: Measures to what extent political elites give public and reasoned justifications for their positions when important policy changes are being considered, i.e. before a decision has been made.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - DelibPartGov. Original data source/s - V-DEM

Definition: Assesses the extent to which citizens feel the last national election was free and fair as well as the extent to which citizens trust the national electoral commission.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - PubPercElecFree. Original data source/s - AFR

Definition: Assesses the extent to which there is freedom of foreign travel and emigration and the extent to which citizens enjoy freedom of movement and residence without restrictions by public authorities.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - PersLibert. Original data source/s - FH/V-DEM/WJP

Definition: Assesses the extent to which citizens enjoy freedom to express personal opinion and academic freedom.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - FreeExpBelief. Original data source/s - FH/V-DEM/WJP

Definition: Assesses the extent to which the political context allows journalists to do their work freely as well as the extent to which media is impartial.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - MediaFree. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/RSF/V-DEM

Definition: Assesses the extent to which there is a legal framework to protect internet users' privacy and their data.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - DigRights. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/DSP&V-DEM/V-DEM

Definition: Assesses the extent to which there is an official mechanism that receives and investigates complaints of ethnic discrimination (whether investigations are undertaken independently or in collaboration with other entities). It also Assesses the extent to which, in the last year, the mechanism has been proactive and implemented programs designed to reduce/combat ethnic discrimination (such as conducting sensitisation campaigns), and the mechanism is accessible to most members of minority ethnic groups.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - ProtAgDiscrim. Original data source/s - AFIDEP

Variable Name: Public Perception of Freedom of Speech
Definition: Assesses the extent to which citizens feel free to say what they think.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - PubFreeSpAFR. Original data source/s - AFR

Definition: Assesses the extent to which political power is distributed according to socioeconomic position. is concerned not with the degree of social inequality but rather with the political eﬀects of this inequality. Speciﬁcally, the extent to which wealth and income translates into political power.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - EqPolPow. Original data source/s - V-DEM

Definition: Assesses to the extent to which various segments of the population (including ethnic, racial, religious, gender, LGBT+, and other relevant groups) have full political rights and electoral opportunities.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - EqPolRep. Original data source/s - FH/IPU/V-DEM

Definition: Estimates the percentage of the total population of the country that lives in the areas where government oﬃcials’ respect for civil liberties is signiﬁcantly weaker than the country average.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - EquCivLib. Original data source/s - V-DEM

Definition: Assesses the extent to which state jobs and state business opportunities are equally open to qualified individuals regardless of socioeconomic position.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - EqSocEconOpp. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/V-DEM

Definition: Assesses the extent to which access to basic public services, such as order and security, primary education, clean water, and healthcare, are distributed equally according to socioeconomic position. Assesses the extent to which the socio-economic position is an important cleavage in society for the distribution of public services.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - EqAccPubServ. Original data source/s - V-DEM

Definition: Assesses the extent to which citizens feel their ethnic group is treated fairly by the government.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - PubPercIncEq. Original data source/s - AFR

Definition: Assesses the extent to which political power is distributed according to gender.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - PolPowRepWom. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/IPU/V-DEM

Definition: Assesses the extent to which women enjoy the same level of civil liberties as men. Here, civil liberties are understood to include access to justice, private property rights, freedom of movement, and freedom from forced labour.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - EqCivLibWom. Original data source/s - V-DEM/WB

Definition: Assesses the extent to which state jobs and state business opportunities are equally open to qualified individuals or firms regardless of gender.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - SocioEconOppWom. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/V-DEM

Variable Name: Access to Public Services for Women
Definition: Assesses the extent to which access to basic public services, such as order and security, primary education, clean water, and healthcare, are distributed equally according to gender. Assesses if gender is an important cleavage in society for the distribution of public services.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - AccPubServWomVDEM. Original data source/s - V-DEM

Definition: Assesses the extent to which there are laws and regulations on violence against women by assessing whether there is legislation specifically addressing domestic violence, there is legislation on sexual harassment in employment, there are criminal penalties or civil remedies for sexual harassment in employment, whether the law is free of legal provisions that require a married woman to obey her husband, and whether a woman can obtain a judgment of divorce in the same way as a man.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - LawsViolenceWomWB. Original data source/s - WB

Definition: This indicator Assesses the extent to which citizens believe women should have the same chance of being elected to political office as men.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - PubPercFemLeadAFR. Original data source/s - AFR

Definition: Assesses to the extent to which citizens can obtain birth certification upon request and both of the following conditions are met: certification is provided within 30 days or less; and certification is provided for no charge.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - CivREg. Original data source/s - AFIDEP

Definition: Assesses the capacity of a country's national statistical system based on a diagnostic framework thereby assessing three dimensions: methodology, source data, periodicity and timeliness.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - CapStatSys. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/ODW/V-DEM/WB

Definition: Assesses the extent to which appointments to the civil service are merit-based and the extent to which public officials perform their duties rigorously and impartially.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - EffAdmin. Original data source/s - AfDB/AFIDEP/V-DEM/WB

Definition: Measures total non-resource tax revenue, excluding social contributions.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - TaxRevMob. Original data source/s - AfDB/UNU-WIDER/WB

Definition: Assesses the extent to which there is: (i) a comprehensive and credible budget, linked to policy priorities, which in turn are linked to a poverty reduction strategy, (ii) effective financial management systems to ensure that incurred expenditures are consistent with the approved budget, that budgeted revenues are achieved, and that aggregate fiscal control is maintained, (iii) timely and accurate fiscal reporting, including timely and audited public accounts and effective arrangements for follow up and (iv) clear and balanced assignment of expenditures and revenues to each level of government.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - BudgMgmt. Original data source/s - AfDB/WB

Variable Name: Public Perception of Easiness to Obtain an Identity Document
Definition: Assesses citizens' perceptions of how easy it is to obtain the identity documents they needed.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - PubPercPubAdmin. Original data source/s - AFR

Definition: Assesses the extent to which government’s actions and efforts (i) promote free movement of persons and labour and easy right of establishment; and (ii) contribute to regional financial integration.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - RegInteg. Original data source/s - AfDB/AfDB&AUC/UNCTAD

Definition: Assesses to which degree exports and imports of a country are concentrated on a few products rather than being distributed in a more homogenous manner among several products.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - EconDiv. Original data source/s - OEC/UNCTAD

Definition: Assesses the extent to which the legal, regulatory, and policy environments help or hinder private businesses in investing, creating jobs and becoming more productive.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - BusCompReg. Original data source/s - AfDB/BS/WB

Definition: Measures bank branches and ATMs per 100,000 adults.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - AccBankServ. Original data source/s - IMF/WB

Definition: Assesses the extent to which labour-employer relations are generally cooperative.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - LabRel. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/ITUC/WEF

Definition: Measures the percentage of employed persons living in poverty in spite of being employed. Poverty is defined using the international poverty line of US$1.90 per day in purchasing power parity (PPP).
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - SecEmplOpp. Original data source/s - ILO/WB

Definition: Assesses extent to which citizens feel the government is doing well at creating jobs.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - SatEO. Original data source/s - AFR

Definition: Measures the extent of the total road network in km (per km2 of exploitable land area) and the total paved roads (km per 10,000 inhabitants) in a country.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - TranspNet. Original data source/s - AfDB/WEF

Definition: Measures the percentage of the population with access to electricity.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - AccEnergy. Original data source/s - WB/WHO

Definition: Measures the number of subscriptions to a public mobile-telephone service that provide access to the public switched telephone network (PSTN) using cellular technology. It includes the number of postpaid subscriptions and the number of active prepaid accounts, divided by population and multiplied by 100.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - MobComm. Original data source/s - ITU

Definition: Measures the proportion of households that have a computer. A computer refers to a desktop computer, a laptop (portable) computer or a tablet (or similar handheld computer).
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - DigAcc. Original data source/s - ITU/Cable/WB

Definition: Assesses the extent of a country's integration level into global shipping liner networks, using six components: i) the number of scheduled ships calls per week in the country; ii) deployed annual capacity in Twenty-Foot-equivalent Units (TEU): total deployed capacity offered at the country; iii) the number of regular liner shipping services from and to the country; iv) the number of liner shipping companies that provide services from and to the country; v) the average size in TEU of the ships deployed by the scheduled service with the largest average vessel size; vi) the number of the other countries that are connected to the country through direct liner shipping services.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - ShipPostFac. Original data source/s - UNCTAD/UPU

Definition: Assesses the extent to which citizens feel the government is doing well at maintaining roads and bridges.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - SatInfr. Original data source/s - AFR

Definition: Assesses the extent to which access to land is afforded to rural people via the policy framework, which is key to their capacity to undergo rural transformation.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - RurLandWatAcc. Original data source/s - IFAD

Definition: Assesses the extent to which existing agricultural input markets and produce markets are reliably providing value for money to smallholders for inputs and the highest proportion of the retail price for produce.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - RurMarkAccIFAD. Original data source/s - IFAD

Definition: Assesses the extent to which government prioritises strategies for and investment in the rural poor and is actively promoting the development of a robust private sector in rural areas.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - RurEcoSupp. Original data source/s - IFAD

Definition: Assesses the extent to which rural people can organize into autonomous groups.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - RurRepPart. Original data source/s - IFAD

Definition: Estimates how much households in each country are spending on health directly out of pocket. It Estimates the share of out of pocket payment of total current health expenditures.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - AccHealth. Original data source/s - V-DEM/WHO

Definition: Measures the availability of drinking water from an improved source, provided collection time is not more than 30 minutes for a round trip, including queuing.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - AccWaterSanit. Original data source/s - WHO&UNICEF

Definition: Measures the estimated number of deaths from malaria in a given year, calculated by MIF per 100,000 of the population using the World Population Prospects by UNDESA.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - ContComDis. Original data source/s - UNAIDS/WHO

Definition: Measures the age-standardised percentage of DALY's (disability adjusted life years) due to metabolic risks. Disability-Adjusted Life Years (DALYs) are defined as the sum of years lost due to premature death and years lived with disability. DALYs are also defined as years of healthy life lost.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - ContNonComDis. Original data source/s - IHME

Definition: Measures the probability of dying between birth and exact age 5, expressed per 1,000 live births.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - ContChildMatHealth. Original data source/s - UN IGME/UN MMEIG/UNDESA/UNICEF&WHO&WB

Definition: Measures compliance with 1 out of 15 WHO International Health Regulations core capacity scores: Human resources. The revised International Health Regulations (IHR) were adopted in 2005 and entered into force in 2007. Under the IHR, States Parties are obliged to develop and maintain minimum core capacities for surveillance and response, including at points of entry, in order to early detect, assess, notify, and respond to any potential public health events of international concern. The 15 capacities are: C1. Policy, legal and normative instruments to implement IHR, C2. IHR coordination and National IHR Focal Point, C.3. Financing, C4. Laboratory, C5. Surveillance, C6. Human resources, C7. Health emergency management, C8. Health services provision, C9. Infection prevention and control (IPC) C10. Risk communication and community engagement (RCCE), C11. Points of entry (PoEs) and border health, C12. Zoonotic diseases, C13. Food safety, C14. Chemical events, C15. Radiation emergencies.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - HealthQual. Original data source/s - WHO

Definition: Assesses citizens' perceptions of how easy it is to obtain the medical care they need.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - SatHealth. Original data source/s - AFR

Definition: Measures the ratio of female gross enrolment to male gross enrolment in primary and lower secondary education.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - EquEduc. Original data source/s - V-DEM/UNESCO

Definition: Measures the number of students enrolled in pre-primary level of education, regardless of age, expressed as a percentage of the eligible official school-age population corresponding to pre-primary education.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - EducEnr. Original data source/s - UNESCO

Definition: Measures the percentage of a cohort of children or young people aged 3-5 years above the intended age for the last grade of the primary level of education who have completed that grade.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - CompEduc. Original data source/s - UNESCO

Definition: Measures the average number of pupils per qualified teacher at the primary level of education in a given academic year. A qualified teacher is one who has at least the minimum academic qualifications required for teaching their subjects at the relevant level in a given country in a given academic year. The higher the pupil/qualified teacher ratio, the lower the relative access of pupils to qualified teachers.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - EducRes. Original data source/s - UNESCO

Definition: Assesses the extent to which there are solid institutions for basic, secondary, and tertiary education, as well as for research and development. It Assesses whether education policy is successful in delivering high quality education and training, and research and development receive effective support from the government. It does not focus on expenditures alone, but also on the quality and competitiveness of the education system and the research sector, considering the structure of funding and knowledge providers (public, private and international cooperation), the output of the educational and developmental efforts, e.g. enrolment rates, literacy rates, percentage of people with higher education and number of patent applications.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - EduqQual. Original data source/s - BS/WB/WEF

Definition: Assesses citizens' perceptions of how easy it was to obtain the services they needed from teachers or school officials.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - SatEduc. Original data source/s - AFR

Definition: Assesses the extent to which there are departments or equivalent mandated to ensure social safety nets for the elderly, those with an illness or health conditions and the unemployed.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - SocialSafNet. Original data source/s - AFIDEP/BS

Definition: Assesses the extent to which significant parts of the population are fundamentally excluded from society due to poverty and inequality. It Assesses the level of social exclusion as a determining factor for economic transformation. It refers to structural exclusion and not to the output strength of a country’s economy as reflected in macroeconomic data like unemployment or GDP growth.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - PovRedPol. Original data source/s - AfDB/BS/WB

Definition: Assesses the extent to which the pattern of public expenditures and revenue collection affects the poor and is consistent with national poverty reduction priorities.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - IneqMitig. Original data source/s - AfDB/FFP/WB/WID.World

Definition: Measures the proportion of the urban population living in slums. Slum households are households whose members suffer one or more of the following ‘household deprivations’: i) Lack of access to improved water source, ii) Lack of access to improved sanitation facilities, iii) Lack of sufficient living area, iv)Lack of housing durability and, v) Lack of security of tenure. Estimations do not include security of tenure due to data limitations.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - DecHous. Original data source/s - CAHF/UN-Habitat

Definition: Measures the prevalence of undernourishment, expressed as the probability that a randomly selected individual from the population consumes an amount of calories that is insufficient to cover her/his energy requirement for an active and healthy life.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - FoodSec. Original data source/s - FAO

Variable Name: Public Perception of Social Protection & Lived Poverty
Definition: Assesses citizens' perceptions of their present living conditions.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - LivPovPubPercSocProt. Original data source/s - AFR

Definition: Assesses the extent to which environmental concerns are effectively taken into account in both macro and microeconomic terms. It considers the extent to which externalisation of costs or inadequate time horizons are avoided or restrained by environmental regulation. In macroeconomic terms, it Assesses the extent to which tax and energy policies take ecological goals and Measures into account (e.g. promotion of renewable energies, CO2 reduction goals). In microeconomic terms, it Assesses the extent to which the government sets incentives for environmentally sound consumption and investments to households and companies. A deeply ingrained awareness of the environment or nature in society may serve as a functional equivalent.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - PromEnvSust. Original data source/s - AfDB/BS/WB

Definition: Assesses the extent to which environmental protection authorities enforce environmental laws. This is based on the following criteria:  the extent to which companies comply with the law after notification of violations from environmental protection authorities; firms get audited or inspected by environmental protection authorities as a result of allegations of pollution by neighbours; firms are routinely audited by the environmental protection authorities; it is likely that environmental protection authorities will impose sanctions if violations are detected.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - EnfEnvPol. Original data source/s - WEF/WJP

Definition: Measures the ratio of disability-adjusted life-years (DALYs) to the population per 100,000 people due to air pollution.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - AirQual. Original data source/s - HEI&IHME

Definition: Measures the diversification of agricultural production as well as the extent to which the agricultural area is under certified organic agriculture.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - SusUseLandFor. Original data source/s - FAO/WB/WRI

Definition: Assesses a country’s efforts to achieve 30% protection for all biomes within its borders. It is calculated using the percentage of each country’s biomes covered by protected areas. It is calculated using the proportions of the area of each of a country’s biome types that are covered by protected areas and then constructing a weighted sum of the protection percentages for all biomes within that country. Protection percentages are weighted according to the prevalence of each biome type within that country.
Other Metadata: IIAG Series Id - LandWatBiodiv. Original data source/s - ENACT/WB/Yale&Columbia
